texto: Sometimes the things that happen aren't as important as the things you remember.
explicacao: Citação de Julia Walton sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Sometimes the things that happen aren't as important as the things you remember.” articula reflexao, memory, things em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

frase_original: Sometimes the things that happen aren't as important as the things you remember.

texto: It's a very strange reality when you can't trust yourself. There's no foundation for anything. The faith I might have had in normal things like gravity or logic or love is gone because my mind might not be reading them correctly. You can't possibly know what it means to doubt everything. To walk into a room full of people and pretend that it's empty because you're not actually sure if it is or not.To never feel completely alone even when you are.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Julia Walton: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “It's a very strange reality when you can't trust yourself. There's no foundation for anything. Th…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, doubt, reality pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reality.

frase_original: It's a very strange reality when you can't trust yourself. There's no foundation for anything. The faith I might have had in normal things like gravity or logic or love is gone because my mind might not be reading them correctly. You can't possibly know what it means to doubt everything. To walk into a room full of people and pretend that it's empty because you're not actually sure if it is or not.To never feel completely alone even when you are.
